solutions.solution:
All bulls are radios → All radios are fans → All bulls are fans (...). Hence Some biscuits are buns. III follows, "All chocolates are Anjalis". Also the first statement. Hence, the conclusion is "No bullies are Anjalis".

2.1: No tables [...] No tables on conclusion.

3.5: No other conclusion is possible.

4.4: I and III have a conclusion between "watches" and "books", the conclusion between watches and books our relevant statements are (a) Now, (a) + (b) = A + I → No conclusion. But, I and III make an alternative pair, which is complementary. Hence, either of them follows, i.e. (b) I → O + E → O : Conclusion IV.

4.4: (b) can be converted to "Some blondes are Asians". This pair does give conclusion I → [...] + O]. No other conclusion is possible.

5.2: (a) gives conclusion III [: I + A = I]. Conclusion I + converted to (b), gives conclusion IV [: I + E → O]. [Note: converted form gives No other conclusion.]

6.5: (c) + (a) gives books are spools [: . I + A = I], which may be converted to conclusion IV. Converted form of (c) [which is: Some books] + (b) gives conclusion III. From conclusion II, we get conclusion I, on conversion. Conclusion II and conclusion I follow conclusion IV + (b).

7.2: (c) gives, on conversion, "Some papers are files". This pair plus conclusion IV [: I + E → O]. No other conclusions are valid. But I and E make a complementary pair [ O-pair].

8.1: (a) + converted (c) → Conclusion III [: A + E → E]. Converted (b) + (a)→ Conclusion III [: I + A = I]. Converted (b) + conclusion II → Conclusion IV [: I + E → O]. [Note: Converted (b) is: Some flowers are girls]

9.1: Converted (c) is: No boxes are chocolates]

9.1: [: E + I → O reversed]. Conclusion III [: I + A → I], (b) + (c) → Conclusion I [Note: (c) is an exclusive proposition. When converted it gives I-type proposition. Hence, converted (c) is: All chocolates are Anjalis]
